title: Admiralty Pilots Act 1925
text:
Title 22
Laws of Bermuda Item 10
BERMUDA
1925 : 43
ADMIRALTY PILOTS ACT 1925
ARRANGEMENT OF SECTIONS
1 Interpretation
2 Damages; liability of Admiralty pilot limited to $600
[28 November 1925]
[preamble and words of enactment omitted]
Interpretation
1 In this Act "Admiralty pilot" means the Queen's Harbour Master
or his assistant or a pilot approved by the Queen's Harbour Master,
while acting as a pilot of any privately owned ship when taking such ship
in or out of either of the cambres or basins at Her Majesty's Dockyard at
Ireland Island,
Damages; liability of Admiralty pilot limited to $600
2 If any damage is done to or by any ship when under the charge
of an Admiralty pilot arising from the neglect or lack of skill on the part
of such pilot, then such pilot shall not be liable in damages to any
greater extent than six hundred dollars.
[Amended by:
1951 : 58 ]
1989 Revision 1
